opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: There is a heavy band crushing my chest, and I can't seem to draw a satisfying, deep breath. It came on yesterday after I did a bit too much, and it hasn't let up. I've had long COVID for a while but this feels different and I'm really scared it's my heart.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's been going on for about 24 hours now and it isn't settling, and I'm on my own and frightened.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on yesterday after I overdid it a bit physically
symptom_narrative.duration: about 24 hours and not letting up
symptom_narrative.character: a tight band right across my chest, and a feeling of air hunger like I can't fill my lungs
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: really uncomfortable and frightening, but I can still get my words out
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: across the middle of my chest, sort of everywhere at the front
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting still and resting takes the edge off
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving around, and it nips a bit more when I take a really deep breath
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a dry cough that won't go away, and I feel wiped out and anxious
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get chest and breathing flare-ups with my long COVID but this one feels stronger
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can walk a few steps but I'm mostly just sitting still because the breathlessness scares me

past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: after a COVID infection a while back
past_medical_history.how_managed: just managing the symptoms, pacing myself

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone and feels quite anxious being on her own with this

objective_data_offered.value: I can still talk to you in full sentences, but I keep taking quick, shallow breaths
breathing feels fast and shallow, like I can't get a full breath in
